steps.title: Identify what the passage actually does
steps.content:
The passage describes Henderson's study: she examined artifacts from a Texas site and found that Black female farmworkers used hook-and-eye closures to balance fashionable feminine silhouettes with the demands of physical labor. The final sentence states her conclusion directly. The passage is a neutral description of research findings — it does not evaluate, dispute, or celebrate the scholarship.

Now eliminate each choice:
  • "To describe an unexpected discovery that altered a researcher's view of how rapidly fashions among Black female farmworkers in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century Texas changed during the period": the text says nothing about how quickly fashions changed, and Henderson's finding is not framed as unexpected. ✗
  • "To discuss research that investigated the ways in which Black female farmworkers in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century Texas used fashion practices to resist traditional gender ideals": the text says the women balanced hegemonic ideals, not that they resisted them. ✗
  • "To evaluate a scholarly work that offers explanations for the impact of urban fashion ideals on Black female farmworkers in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century Texas": the passage describes, not evaluates, and the site is agricultural and domestic — not urban. ✗
  • "To summarize the findings of a study that explored factors influencing a fashion practice among Black female farmworkers in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century Texas": the passage summarizes Henderson's findings about two factors — hegemonic fashion ideals and work requirements — that shaped the women's clothing choices. ✓

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
To summarize the findings of a study that explored factors influencing a fashion practice among Black female farmworkers in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century Texas matches what the passage does: it summarizes research into the factors that shaped a specific fashion practice among Black female farmworkers.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
